decisions.reasoning: Market is closed (Saturday). First journal entry — bootstrapping state. Current positions: AAPL 5sh avg $312.60 (-0.17%), MSFT 3sh avg $425.31 (+5.86%), NVDA 16sh avg $217.20 (-2.79%), QQQ 1sh avg $731.84 (+0.88%), SPY 3sh avg $733.31 (+3.16%). All SMA signals are long. NVDA is the main drag with unrealized loss of ~$97; prior session trimmed 5 shares on May-28, leaving 16sh. Cash is $667.50 (~6.7% of equity). No action warranted today.
decisions.invalidation: Any single position breaching -8% unrealized loss triggers review on next market open

summary: Market closed (Saturday); first journal bootstrap. All 5 positions held. NVDA -$97 is the key watch item for Monday.
open_theses: NVDA: trimmed from 21→16sh on May-28; avg entry $217.20 vs close $211.14 (-2.79%). Will reassess Mon Jun-01 with fresh news; invalidation if AI capex cycle slows or further gap-down.
MSFT: strongest performer at +5.86%; holding for continued AI/cloud momentum. Watch for any guidance revision risk at next earnings.
SPY+QQQ: broad index core. All SMA signals long (SPY fast 745 vs slow 730, QQQ fast 719 vs slow 691). Macro tail remains constructive unless FOMC or jobs data surprise hawkishly.
AAPL: added 1sh May-29 bringing to 5sh; near breakeven (-0.17%). Watching for WWDC product announcements and any iPhone demand updates in Jun.
